behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff plus auth check, not just pure HMAC math. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of `verifySignature()` misses the real bug where handler still forwards on mismatch. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process request/response and fake queue are sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network conditions, only status code and forwarding suppression.
behaviors.test_description: Wire HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue adapter in-process. Send request with invalid `X-Signature-256` over a test server/client harness, assert 401, and assert queue `enqueue` was not called / no record persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (handler, persistence-backed queue, response writer). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing handler branches alone can miss misordered await/commit logic. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process app + local persistent queue implementation; no external network/subprocess needed. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text requires write-before-ack ordering, not real network faults.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP invocation against handler wired to real local persistent queue (test DB/file). Instrument response completion timestamp and queue commit timestamp; assert commit happens first, then 200 is sent.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at boundary between enqueue/dispatch and async processor lifecycle. Need module wiring to catch accidental awaiting of downstream promise. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network dependency is necessary; delayed in-process processor is enough. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because text does not require real infrastructure behavior, only non-blocking control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker stub that blocks for a long duration. Send valid event and assert HTTP response arrives before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets wall-clock SLA under concurrency pressure, which depends on real event-loop scheduling/timing and HTTP serving behavior. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers or mocked transport can pass while real server stalls under contention. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because wording requires wall-clock and concurrent slow prior events, a known Integration->System top-loss boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run real Node HTTP service process, generate concurrent traffic where prior events trigger slow downstream work, and measure real client-observed latency for new requests; assert p95/each tested response under 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is state handoff between handler and persistent queue/idempotency store. Unit tests of a single dedupe helper miss race/order interactions with persistence writes. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store wiring is enough; no real network/subprocess required. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require OS/network effects beyond local module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: With handler wired to real test persistence, submit same event ID twice and assert only one queued record exists and second request follows expected idempotent response path.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is contract mapping from queue backpressure signal to HTTP response semantics. Needs handler+queue adapter wiring; pure unit of response formatter is too narrow. REAL-INFRA: queue-full can be produced by in-process fake/real local queue, so System is not required for this specific behavior. REJECTION-GATE: higher level rejected because text checks mapping behavior, not real capacity mechanics (covered by behavior 7).
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue adapter that deterministically returns `full`. Send valid request and assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header present with configured value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component + persistence backend interaction (stateful capacity enforcement), beyond a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local DB/filesystem queue backend in-process; no external network/subprocess needed. REJECTION-GATE: System considered but rejected because behavior does not mention OS-level failure modes, only local capacity enforcement logic in persistence layer.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend with small configured capacity, enqueue until full, then enqueue one more event and assert explicit rejection/error without increasing stored count.
